title: galium aparine var. aparinella
intro: Galium aparine var. aparinella, commonly known as cleavers, is a perennial plant with sticky stems and leaves, native to North America. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antiviral effects. The health benefits of cleavers include its ability to support the immune system, reduce swelling and pain, and aid in the treatment of skin conditions such as acne and eczema. Cleavers can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, and is often used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
